name: Emer Cooke
role: Executive Director of the European Medicines Agency (EMA)
bio: Emer Cooke is an Irish pharmacist who has served as the Executive Director of the European Medicines Agency since November 2020.
_evidence: As head of the EMA, she led the regulatory review that ultimately reaffirmed the vaccine's safety, directly countering the suspension decisions made by member states.

name: Pascal Soriot
bio: Pascal Soriot is a French business executive who has served as the Chief Executive Officer of AstraZeneca since 2012.
_evidence: As CEO, he was the primary public face of the company, responsible for defending the vaccine's safety profile and managing the reputational fallout during the March 2021 crisis.

name: Ann Taylor
bio: Ann Taylor serves as the Chief Medical Officer at AstraZeneca, overseeing the company's clinical development and safety monitoring.
_evidence: She provided the medical and scientific justification for the vaccine's safety, citing data from millions of vaccinated individuals to refute claims of increased clotting risks.
